objective: Validate that carto rm command correctly deletes a node and all its edges, matches the 8 acceptance criteria, and passes all 28 tests
key_changes: AC1: removeNode() filters by id for both nodes and edges (from/to) — correct cascading delete
AC2: CLI outputs exact string '✓ Removed "%s" and %d edge(s)' in chalk.green
AC3: Not-found: chalk.red + console.error + process.exit(1) — exact match
AC4: npm run build: 0 TypeScript errors
AC5: npm test: 28/28 passed (3 new removeNode tests + 25 existing)
AC7: All relative imports use explicit .js extensions — convention compliant
AC8: graph.ts is pure functions; all I/O in cli.ts — separation preserved
Edge case: not-found → null covered; orphan node covered; cascade delete covered
Regression: CART-B01 test untouched; all 24 existing tests still pass
Test quality: assertions are specific (node.id, edgeCount, persisted graph state inspection)
